backlog.description: Engram — trustable institutional memory for multi-agent AI teams. Postgres+pgvector service, FastAPI, multi-tenant, with review states, provenance, conflict detection, and scoped recall.
backlog.current_state: v0.1.0 skeleton with v2 schema (trust fields, review states, RLS, FTS, separate embeddings table, item_events audit). Migration SQL, ORM models, FastAPI app factory, route stubs (NotImplementedError), Docker Compose, test scaffolding exist. No endpoint is functional yet.

conventions.commit_style: conventional commits (feat:, fix:, test:, docs:, refactor:)
conventions.pr_per_task: one branch, one PR per task. Do not bundle unrelated changes.
conventions.rls_note: The application must set app.tenant_id and app.principal_id per DB session via SET LOCAL. The get_session dependency should do this based on the authenticated principal.
conventions.check_constraints: All enum-like fields (kind, visibility, review_status, source_type, sensitivity, conflict_type) have CHECK constraints in the DB. Pydantic models should use Literal types to validate before the DB does.
conventions.dedup_scope: Dedup is (tenant_id, workspace_id, principal_id, content_hash) WHERE valid_to IS NULL AND review_status != 'rejected'. Enforced by unique index idx_memitems_dedup.
conventions.append_first: Content is never UPDATEd. Metadata changes (wing, room, visibility, review_status) go through PATCH which writes to item_events audit table then updates the column. Content changes require supersession (new row + mark old).

milestones.name: Canonical memory MVP
milestones.description: The minimum that makes Engram usable: schema verified, remember with trust fields, startup recall with scoring, keyword+semantic search, item CRUD, CCA export, CCA import, Docker deployment. No KG, no LLM classification, no conflict detection.

milestones.name: Trust and classification
milestones.description: LLM-backed classification, review workflow, conflict detection, recall explanations, feedback endpoint, MemPalace import.

milestones.name: Graph and navigation
milestones.description: KG triples with visibility inheritance, tunnels, taxonomy browser, diary, memory hygiene.

milestones.name: Hermes integration
milestones.description: SDK, MCP adapter, engram-hooks companion library, retire old stores.

milestones.name: Deployment + ops
milestones.description: Provision Proxmox VM, deploy, verify over Tailscale.

tasks.title: Verify v2 schema applies cleanly and health/ready endpoints work
tasks.description: Stand up Docker Compose, confirm migrations/001_init.sql (v2) applies without errors. The schema now has 12 tables, CHECK constraints, RLS policies, FTS trigger, and seeded data. Verify health/ready endpoints and RLS session context.
acceptance_criteria: docker compose up -d succeeds without errors

All 13 tables exist: tenants, workspaces, principals, workspace_members, memory_items, memory_embeddings, kg_triples, tunnels, item_events, classification_rules, deletion_events, api_keys, recall_logs
CHECK constraints are active (try inserting invalid kind/visibility/review_status and confirm rejection)
RLS policies are active: INSERT a memory_item without setting app.tenant_id → should fail or be invisible
FTS trigger works: INSERT a memory_item, verify content_tsv is populated
pgvector extension is active with version >= 0.8 (check SELECT extversion FROM pg_extension WHERE extname='vector')
Seed data exists: default tenant, general workspace, admin principal, workspace membership, 6 classification rules
GET /health returns ok, GET /ready returns ready with database connected
tasks.context_notes: The db.py get_session dependency must set app.tenant_id and app.principal_id via SET LOCAL at the start of each session, based on the authenticated principal. For Phase 1A with auth disabled, it should set the default tenant/admin. The pgvector/pgvector:pg16 Docker image includes pgvector but verify the version — iterative_scan requires 0.8+. The RLS policies reference current_setting('app.tenant_id', true) — test that this works correctly with SET LOCAL.

tasks.title: Implement content canonicalization and hashing utilities
tasks.description: Create the canonicalization module. Same as before but now the hash is used in the unique dedup index idx_memitems_dedup scoped to (tenant, workspace, principal).
acceptance_criteria: engram/canonicalize.py exports canonicalize(content: str) -> str and content_hash(canonical: str) -> str
canonicalize() strips leading/trailing whitespace, collapses internal whitespace, lowercases
content_hash() returns 'sha256:' + hex digest
Two semantically identical strings produce the same hash
All tests pass
tasks.context_notes: Pure functions, no DB dependency. The hash feeds into the unique index idx_memitems_dedup, so it must be deterministic and stable across releases.

tasks.title: Implement POST /v1/remember — the canonical write path with trust fields
tasks.description: Implement the remember endpoint with the full trust model: content canonicalization, dedup via unique index, review_status assignment based on source_type, source_trust defaults, sensitivity check (secret denylist), and embedding generation (deferred to T05). This is the most important endpoint — it's where the trust model lives.
acceptance_criteria: POST /v1/remember creates a memory_item with all trust fields populated
source_type='manual' (user explicitly said remember) → review_status='active', source_trust=0.9
source_type='extraction' or 'sync_turn' → review_status='proposed', source_trust=0.4-0.5
source_type='import' or 'migration' → review_status='active', source_trust=0.8
Dedup: same (tenant, workspace, principal, content_hash) with valid_to IS NULL → returns status='deduped', no new row
Dedup respects review_status: a rejected item doesn't block re-creation
content_hash is computed via canonicalize module
subject_type/subject_id/subject_name stored if provided
sensitivity defaults to 'normal'; secret-pattern denylist blocks content matching API key/token patterns (return 422)
external_id/external_source stored if provided (for imports)
embedding is null (deferred to T05); a placeholder row in memory_embeddings may be created with status='pending'
Returns {id, status: created|deduped|superseded, review_status, memory_confidence}
source_trust and review_status computed from (source_type, principal.type) — manual from user = 0.9/active, manual from agent = 0.6/proposed
tasks.context_notes: Create engram/safety.py with the secret-pattern denylist (regex patterns for AWS keys, GitHub tokens, generic API keys, passwords). The source_trust and review_status defaults are defined in design.md Section 4 (Trust Model) — encode them as a lookup table. The unique index idx_memitems_dedup will raise IntegrityError on duplicate — catch it and return status='deduped'. For supersession: if kind is 'preference' or 'invariant' (singleton semantics), and a new item with same family key is written, mark old item's valid_to + superseded_by. The RememberRequest model in memory.py needs updating to include the new fields (subject_type, subject_id, subject_name, importance, sensitivity, external_id, external_source). The source_trust/review_status defaults now depend on BOTH source_type and principal.type. Encode the lookup table from design.md Section 4 (Source trust defaults). This prevents a random agent calling /remember manually from getting the same trust as a user.

tasks.title: Implement POST /v1/recall — startup mode with scoring and explanations
tasks.description: Implement startup recall with the full scoring formula from design.md Section 4. Returns active items ordered by score, bounded by budget, with per-item 'reasons' explaining why each was included.
acceptance_criteria: POST /v1/recall with mode='startup' returns active items (review_status='active', valid_to IS NULL)
Items scored by formula: importance*0.25 + source_trust*0.20 + memory_confidence*0.20 + recency*0.15 + verified*0.10 + pinned*0.10
Pinned items always included first (regardless of score), then scorer fills remainder of budget
byte_budget or token_budget enforced (token approximated as bytes/4)
working_set rendered as compact text (one line per item)
Each item includes 'reasons' array: ['kind=invariant', 'human verified', 'pinned', etc.]
recall_logs entry written with item_ids for feedback loop
recall_count incremented and last_recalled_at updated on returned items
Deterministic: same corpus + same budget = same output
Pinned items capped at max_pinned_tokens (config, default 2048); excess dropped with pinned_omitted_count in response
Anti-feedback loop: startup_recall_count tracked; after threshold (default 5) without positive feedback, recency bonus reduced by penalty_factor
tasks.context_notes: Create engram/recall.py with the scoring as a pure function for testability. The recency_bonus should be a decay function — something like max(0, 1 - days_since_last_recall/30). The reasons array should be human-readable strings explaining the score components. The RecallRequest/RecallResponse models in memory.py need updating to include token_budget and the per-item reasons. IMPORTANT: only review_status='active' items enter startup recall — proposed items do not. This is the trust gate.

tasks.title: Implement embeddings (separate table) and POST /v1/search (keyword + semantic + hybrid)
tasks.description: Implement the embedding generation pipeline (writing to memory_embeddings table) and the search endpoint with three modes. Keyword uses FTS (content_tsv + GIN index), semantic uses pgvector with iterative_scan, hybrid combines both.
acceptance_criteria: When embedding_provider != 'none', new memory items get an embedding row in memory_embeddings with embedding_model='text-embedding-3-small'
Embedding model and dimension are tracked in the row (not assumed)
POST /v1/search mode='keyword' uses FTS: SELECT ... WHERE content_tsv @@ plainto_tsquery(:query)
POST /v1/search mode='semantic' joins memory_embeddings, uses cosine distance (<=>), sets hnsw.iterative_scan=strict
POST /v1/search mode='hybrid' combines FTS + semantic with reciprocal rank fusion
Only active items (review_status='active', valid_to IS NULL) returned by default
Search respects visibility scoping via RLS
If no embeddings exist, semantic search returns empty results with helpful message
tasks.context_notes: The memory_embeddings table is separate from memory_items — the search query must JOIN them. For pgvector queries: MemoryEmbedding.embedding.cosine_distance(query_vector) or raw SQL. CRITICAL: set hnsw.iterative_scan=strict at query time (SET LOCAL hnsw.iterative_scan = strict) to handle tenant-filtered queries without recall degradation. The openai package needs to be added to dependencies if not present. text-embedding-3-small produces 1536-dim vectors matching the vector(1536) column.

tasks.title: Implement item CRUD, PATCH with audit, review/verify endpoints
tasks.description: Implement item listing (cursor pagination), detail view, PATCH metadata (with item_events audit), supersede, invalidate, review status change, and verify endpoints.
acceptance_criteria: GET /v1/items uses cursor pagination (not offset) — cursor is the last item's created_at + id
GET /v1/items/{id} returns full detail including item_events history and linked KG facts (if any)
PATCH /v1/items/{id} updates metadata (wing, room, visibility, importance, pinned), writes an item_event row, THEN updates the column
PATCH returns the updated item + the event record
POST /v1/items/{id}/supersede creates new item, marks old valid_to + superseded_by
POST /v1/items/{id}/invalidate sets valid_to=now()
POST /v1/items/{id}/review changes review_status, writes item_event (event_type='review_change')
POST /v1/items/{id}/verify sets human_verified=True, verified_by, verified_at, writes item_event
404 for non-existent IDs
tasks.context_notes: Create engram/api/routes/review.py for the review/verify/conflict endpoints. The PATCH endpoint is the key append-first reconciliation: content is never touched, but metadata changes are legitimate operational updates that must be audited. The item_events table captures: event_type, field_name, old_value, new_value, actor, reason. Cursor pagination: use (created_at, id) compound cursor for stable ordering. The review endpoint is the trust gate — moving from proposed to active is the human/automated review action.

tasks.title: Implement CCA export and CCA importer
tasks.description: Export the CCA ledger view to git-friendly JSON, and import existing CCA JSON ledger into Engram.
acceptance_criteria: GET /v1/export/cca returns JSON matching cca_lite_memory_packet@v1 format
Only active items (valid_to IS NULL) with kind IN (doctrine, decision, invariant, preference) included
Export includes trust fields: review_status, memory_confidence, source_trust, human_verified
scripts/import_cca.py reads --cca-file and --engram-url
Dry-run mode (default): reports count by kind, duplicates detected
--apply mode: imports via POST /v1/remember with source_type='migration', external_source='cca'
captured_at timestamps preserved as valid_from
content_hash from CCA used for dedup detection
tasks.context_notes: Reference: /home/zutfen/code/cca-repo/docs/cca-lite/hermes-memory.json. ~40 reviewed entries. The importer should use the REST API (httpx), not direct DB access. Kind mapping: 'decision'→'decision', 'invariant'→'invariant', 'operational_fact'→'fact', 'preference'→'preference'.

tasks.title: Implement LLM-backed classification with auto-classify on remember
tasks.description: Complete the classification engine with LLM enrichment. When provider='openai', an LLM call refines rule-based classification with semantic understanding. Wire auto-classification into remember when kind is omitted.
acceptance_criteria: POST /v1/classify returns suggested_kind, suggested_wing, suggested_room, confidence, reason, rules_matched
Rule-based classification works without LLM (provider='none')
LLM-enriched classification when provider='openai': prompt includes tenant's kind taxonomy + wing/room vocabulary
confidence: rule-only 0.6-0.8, LLM-enriched 0.7-0.95
POST /v1/remember calls classify() when kind is omitted; explicit kind always overrides
Auto-classified provenance stored (which rules/LLM led to the classification)
tasks.context_notes: The LLM prompt should ask for structured JSON output. Include the kind taxonomy, existing wing/room vocabulary (queried from DB), and the content. Use the openai package. classification_provider, classification_model, classification_confidence_threshold are in config.py. IMPORTANT over-promotion lesson: be conservative. Default to kind='fact' with lower confidence when uncertain. The companion library (T18) applies promotion gates using the confidence score.

tasks.title: Implement conflict detection at write time
tasks.description: On remember, run semantic similarity check against active items in scope. Above threshold, ask classifier: contradict, refine, or duplicate? Flag conflicts, auto-supersede refinements, auto-dedup duplicates.
acceptance_criteria: POST /v1/remember checks for semantic similarity against active items in same (tenant, workspace, kind)
If cosine similarity > 0.85: classifier determines duplicate/refine/contradict
duplicate → auto-dedup (return existing item)
refine → auto-supersede (mark old, write new, link via superseded_by)
contradict → flag conflict: set conflicts_with_item_id, conflict_type='contradiction', conflict_resolution_status='unresolved', review_status='proposed'
GET /v1/review/conflicts lists items with unresolved conflicts
POST /v1/items/{id}/resolve-conflict accepts resolution (accept/reject/merge), sets conflict_resolution_status, writes item_event
tasks.context_notes: Create engram/conflicts.py. The similarity check uses the same embeddings from T05 — requires embeddings to be populated. If embedding_provider='none', conflict detection is skipped (can't do semantic comparison). The classifier prompt for contradict/refine/duplicate should include both the old and new content. Conflict detection is a major differentiator — it turns temporal validity from passive bookkeeping into active hygiene.

tasks.title: Implement recall explanations and feedback endpoint
tasks.description: Enhance recall responses with per-item 'reasons' and 'warnings', and add POST /v1/feedback for marking recalled items as useful/noise to feed back into recall scoring.
acceptance_criteria: Recall response items include 'reasons' array explaining score components
Recall response items include 'warnings' array (e.g., 'not confirmed in 90 days', 'low confidence')
POST /v1/feedback accepts {item_id, feedback: useful|noise} and adjusts importance over time
Useful feedback incrementally raises importance (capped at 0.95); noise lowers it (floor at 0.1)
Feedback is logged
tasks.context_notes: The reasons array was designed into T04 but this task fleshes out the warning system and the feedback loop. Warnings: items not recalled in 90 days, items with memory_confidence < 0.5, items with unresolved conflicts. The feedback endpoint closes the loop that recall_logs started — items that get recalled and marked useful earn priority.

tasks.title: Implement MemPalace importer
tasks.description: Import drawers from MemPalace ChromaDB into Engram. Dry-run first.
acceptance_criteria: Reads --palace path (ChromaDB dir) and --engram-url
Dry-run: reports count by wing/room, duplicates, manual review items
--apply: imports each drawer via POST /v1/remember with source_type='migration', external_source='mempalace'
Wing/room preserved, kind inferred
KG triples and tunnels imported via /v1/kg and /v1/tunnels
tasks.context_notes: MemPalace store at ~/.mempalace/palace/. ~800-900 drawers. Use chromadb Python package to read. Use REST API for writing. Kind inference: 'doctrine'/'decision' in source_file → kind='doctrine', diary → 'diary_entry', else 'fact'.

tasks.title: Implement API key authentication and workspace membership
tasks.description: API key auth middleware, workspace membership enforcement, and admin endpoints for tenant/workspace/principal management.
acceptance_criteria: ENGRAM_AUTH_ENABLED=false → bypass auth, use default tenant/admin
ENGRAM_AUTH_ENABLED=true → Bearer token required, resolves to tenant + principal
API keys stored hashed (bcrypt), scopes enforced (read/write/admin/export)
Workspace membership checked for workspace-scoped queries
POST /v1/admin/tenants, /v1/admin/workspaces, /v1/admin/principals, /v1/admin/api-keys
RLS session context set from authenticated principal
Health endpoints exempt from auth
tasks.context_notes: The get_current_principal dependency reads Authorization header, validates key, returns tenant_id + principal_id + scopes. get_session must SET LOCAL app.tenant_id and app.principal_id based on the authenticated principal for RLS. Key format: 'eng_<random>'. CLI command for key generation.

tasks.title: Implement KG endpoints with visibility inheritance
tasks.description: Implement KG add/query/invalidate/timeline. Visibility inherited from source_item_id at query time — a triple spawned from a private item is only visible to that principal.
acceptance_criteria: POST /v1/kg creates triple, links to source_item_id if provided
GET /v1/kg/query checks source_item visibility: if source item is private, triple only visible to owning principal
GET /v1/kg/query supports entity, direction, as_of, predicate filters
GET /v1/kg/timeline returns chronological facts
POST /v1/kg/invalidate marks valid_to
All queries RLS-scoped to tenant
tasks.context_notes: The visibility inheritance is the fix for Claude's headline finding. At query time, JOIN kg_triples to memory_items on source_item_id and check visibility. Triples without a source_item_id default to workspace visibility. This prevents the KG from being a side-channel around the visibility model.

tasks.title: Implement taxonomy, tunnels, and diary endpoints
tasks.description: Taxonomy browser (wing→room→count), tunnel CRUD, diary write/read.
acceptance_criteria: GET /v1/taxonomy returns nested wing→room→item_count (active items only)
GET/POST /v1/tunnels for cross-wing links
POST /v1/diary creates memory_item kind='diary_entry', visibility='private'
GET /v1/diary/{principal} returns diary entries
tasks.context_notes: Taxonomy is a GROUP BY query. Diaries are memory_items with locked defaults. See mempalace-setup skill for prior art.

tasks.title: Implement memory hygiene tools (stale detection, archival)
tasks.description: Endpoints for detecting stale memories (not recalled in N days), bulk archival, and hygiene reports.
acceptance_criteria: GET /v1/review/stale?days=90 returns active items not recalled in N days
POST /v1/items/bulk-archive accepts item_ids, sets review_status='archived'
GET /v1/review/stats returns counts by review_status, kind, confidence buckets
tasks.context_notes: Stale detection uses last_recalled_at. Archival excludes items from default recall without invalidating them (they're still true, just not actively useful). This addresses the diary/observation bloat concern — append-first plus no decay means recall degrades over time without hygiene.

tasks.title: Build Python SDK client
tasks.description: Thin async Python SDK wrapping the REST API.
acceptance_criteria: EngramClient(base_url, api_key) with async methods: remember, recall, search, classify, kg_add, kg_query, diary_write
Context manager support
Graceful error handling
tasks.context_notes: Separate package in sdk/engram-client/. Depends only on httpx + pydantic.

tasks.title: Build MCP server adapter
tasks.description: MCP server exposing Engram tools. Replaces MemPalace MCP server.
acceptance_criteria: Tools: engram_remember, engram_recall, engram_search, engram_classify, engram_kg_query, engram_kg_add, engram_diary_write
Config via ENGRAM_BASE_URL, ENGRAM_API_KEY env vars
README with Hermes config example
tasks.context_notes: Uses the Python SDK. Tool names prefixed with 'engram_'. See mempalace-setup skill for MCP config format.

tasks.title: Build engram-hooks companion library (Hermes lifecycle hooks)
tasks.description: Package the Hermes lifecycle hooks (pre_compress, sync_turn, session_end, write-boundary guards) as a standalone library calling Engram's classify + remember. Successor to zutfen_memory plugin.
acceptance_criteria: pre_compress, sync_turn, session_end hooks extract facts, classify via Engram, route by confidence
Write-boundary guard blocks ambiguous/ephemeral candidates
Promotion gates: low-confidence → volatile, high-confidence → remember as proposed
Volatile recall: local file-backed, 14-day retention, 2000-entry cap
Config maps Hermes lifecycle events to hooks
tasks.context_notes: Direct successor to zutfen_memory. The skill at ~/.hermes/skills/software-development/zutfen-memory-routing/ is the spec. Classification is delegated to Engram (T08); lifecycle decisions stay here. The volatile layer stays local. The write-boundary guard must actively reject (return {handled: True}), not passthrough (return None).

tasks.title: Provision Proxmox VM and deploy Engram service
tasks.description: Provision dedicated VM, deploy via Docker Compose, verify over Tailscale.
acceptance_criteria: VM on Proxmox (4 vCPU, 8GB RAM, 50GB disk)
Docker Compose up succeeds, schema migrates
curl http://<tailscale-ip>:8000/health returns ok
Nightly pg_dump backup configured
Bootstrap API key created
docs/deployment.md documents full process
tasks.context_notes: Use proxmox-vm-provisioning skill. Tailscale network. User prefers SSH bootstrap with local key generation. 8GB RAM for HNSW index + Postgres.

parallelizable_groups.description: After T01+T02+T03 land, these proceed in parallel:

backlog.critical_path_note: T01→T02→T03 is the serial bottleneck. After T03, work fans out. T04 (recall) and T08 (classification) are both on the critical path because the SDK (T16) depends on both. T16→T17 is the integration path. T18 (engram-hooks) can proceed in parallel with T17 once T16+T08 land.
